primary_diagnosis.name: Post-infectious IBS / mast cell overlap flare with volume depletion — surgical/infective abdomen to be excluded

pathophysiology_summary: Post-infectious IBS (with mast cell reactivity) develops after gastroenteritis, producing visceral hypersensitivity, dysmotility, cramping and secretory diarrhoea, with flares triggered by food/stress. Severe flares cause large fluid losses with tachycardia and presyncope. This mimics a surgical abdomen (appendicitis/obstruction/peritonitis) and infectious enterocolitis. Reassuring features are a soft non-peritonitic abdomen with passage of stool/flatus, no GI bleeding, afebrile status with no contacts, no chronic alarm features, and a reproducible prior pattern. However, presyncope, tachycardia, dry mucosa and inability to keep fluids down indicate significant dehydration, and telehealth cannot examine the abdomen, measure BP, or give IV fluids. The correct action is urgent in-person/ED assessment for an abdominal review and IV rehydration — not remote injections — with 000 for a rigid abdomen, GI bleeding or true collapse.

differential.evidence_basis: Crampy pain/diarrhoea with visceral hypersensitivity on a post-infectious background.
Severe pain demands exclusion of a surgical cause.

differential.diagnosis: Post-infectious IBS/mast cell flare with dehydration
differential.evidence_basis: Soft abdomen, no bleeding, afebrile, reproducible pattern — a functional flare, but significantly dehydrated.
Lower probability but requires in-person confirmation.
Afebrile lowers probability; stool studies if features suggest.
The active concern requiring IV rehydration.

differential.diagnosis: Post-infectious IBS/mast cell flare with volume depletion
differential.evidence_basis: Working telehealth position: functional flare with dehydration needing urgent in-person/ED assessment (abdominal review + IV fluids), not remote injections.

red_flags.description: Presyncope, tachycardia, dry mucosa and inability to keep fluids down (significant dehydration)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Volume depletion risks collapse and AKI, worsened by isolation.

red_flags.description: Rigid board-like abdomen, or blood in stool/vomit
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would indicate a surgical abdomen or significant GI bleeding.

red_flags.description: Chronic alarm features (weight loss, persistent bleeding) or fever

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Attributing everything to IBS/mast cells and missing a surgical abdomen or significant dehydration.
Advising home management despite presyncope, tachycardia and inability to keep fluids down while isolated.
Arranging IM antispasmodic/antiemetic over chat instead of assessment/rehydration.
Assuming a soft abdomen now excludes an evolving surgical process without arranging re-examination.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Clinically dehydrated with presyncope/tachycardia; unable to maintain oral intake
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports urgent in-person/ED assessment with IV fluids and abdominal review.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Blood pressure/postural BP and objective hydration status
IV rehydration and bloods (U&E, FBC, CRP)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Screen surgical/infective features; arrange urgent in-person/ED assessment for an abdominal review and IV rehydration; escalate to 000 for a rigid abdomen, GI bleeding or true collapse.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Managing remotely as a 'gut flare' could miss a surgical abdomen or leave a dehydrated isolated patient to collapse.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Severe abdominal pain with systemic features requires in-person assessment to exclude a surgical cause and manage dehydration; IBS is a diagnosis of exclusion requiring alarm-feature review.
Post-infectious IBS is managed symptomatically once organic causes are excluded; acute severe flares with dehydration warrant assessment.
